IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: NOVO NORDISK FONDEN (NNF) CONTRIBUTES SIGNIFICANTLY TO RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT THAT IMPROVES THE LIVES OF PEOPLE AND THE SUSTAINABILITY OF SOCIETY THROUGH GRANTMAKING.
IRS990.MissionDesc: NOVO NORDISK FONDEN (NNF) CONTRIBUTES SIGNIFICANTLY TO RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT THAT IMPROVES THE LIVES OF PEOPLE AND THE SUSTAINABILITY OF SOCIETY THROUGH GRANTMAKING.
IRS990.Desc: NNF FOCUSES ITS GRANTMAKING PROGRAM TOWARD 3 MAIN OBJECTIVES:1. TO SUPPORT PHYSIOLOGICAL, ENDOCRINOLOGICAL, METABOLIC, AND OTHER MEDICAL RESEARCH. FOR 2024, 25% OF GRANTS WERE AWARDED FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH.2. TO SUPPORT RESEARCH HOSPITAL ACTIVITIES RELATED TO DIABETES IN DENMARK. FOR 2024, 0% OF GRANTS WERE AWARDED FOR RESEARCH HOSPITAL ACTIVITIES.3. TO SUPPORT OTHER SCIENTIFIC, HUMANITARIAN, AND SOCIAL PURPOSES. FOR 2024, 75% OF GRANTS WERE AWARDED FOR SUCH OTHER PURPOSES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: NOVO HOLDINGS HOLDS AND MAINTAINS ENDOWMENT FUNDS IN ORDER TO FURTHER NOVO NORDISK FONDEN'S GRANTMAKING PROGRAMS RELATED TO MEDICAL RESEARCH, RESEARCH HOSPITAL ACTIVITIES, AND OTHER HUMANITARIAN AND SOCIAL PURPOSES.
SLA SHARED SERVICES AND FX ADJUSTMENT 7,715,159.
SLA SHARED SERVICES AND FX ADJUSTMENT 7,687,021.

AccountActivitiesOutsideUSGrp.RegionTxt: EAST ASIA AND THE PACIFIC - AUSTRALIA, BRUNEI, BURMA, CAMBODIA,
EUROPE (INCLUDING ICELAND & GREENLAND) - ALBANIA, ANDORRA, AUSTRIA, BELGIUM
NORTH AMERICA - CANADA AND MEXICO, BUT NOT THE UNITED STATES
SOUTH ASIA - AFGHANISTAN, BANGLADESH, BHUTAN, INDIA, MALDIVES, NEPAL,
SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA - ANGOLA, BENIN, BOTSWANA, BURKINA FASO,

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: NNF'S GRANTS ARE PARTLY AWARDED THROUGH OPEN CALLS AND PARTLY THROUGH STRATEGIC ONE-OFF INITIATIVES THAT ARE APPROVED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. NNF AWARDS GRANTS IN OPEN COMPETITION WITHIN THE SEVEN GRANT-AWARDING FOCUS STRATEGIES. ALL GRANTS AWARDED IN OPEN COMPETITION ARE PEER REVIEWED BY COMMITTEES THAT HAVE BEEN ESTABLISHED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THESE 24 COMMITTEES ARE COMPRISED OF 5 TO 12 MEMBERS WHO ARE INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED EXPERTS IN THEIR FIELD, AND WHO EVALUATE THE GRANTS BASED ON QUALITY, NOVELTY, POTENTIAL IMPACT, BUDGET, AND QUALIFICATIONS OF THE APPLICANT. FOR A LIST OF THESE COMMITTEES, SEE HTTPS://NOVONORDISKFONDEN.DK/EN/COMMITTEES. EACH COMMITTEE EVALUATES EACH APPLICATION ASSIGNED TO IT VERY CAREFULLY. FIRST, THE MEMBERS OF THE COMMITTEE EVALUATE THE APPLICATIONS INDIVIDUALLY AND GIVE THEM A SCORE. AFTER THIS, THE COMMITTEE MEETS TO DISCUSS THE HIGHEST-RANKING APPLICATIONS TO REACH CONSENSUS ON GRANTING RECOMMENDATIONS. THE EVALUATION PROCESS OF SOME CALLS MAY INCLUDE AN ADDITIONAL ELABORATED APPLICATION, A PERSONAL INTERVIEW WITH THE APPLICANT, OR THE USE OF FURTHER EXPERT PEER REVIEW. NNF POSTS ITS OPEN COMPETITION CALLS FOR APPLICATIONS HERE - HTTPS://NOVONORDISKFONDEN.DK/EN/GRANT/ - AND INTERESTED APPLICANTS APPLY ON-LINE USING NORMA, NNF'S APPLICATION AND GRANT MANAGEMENT SYSTEM. THE ENTIRE EVALUATION PROCESS IS CLOSELY MONITORED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS, WHICH HAS SET RULES OF PROCEDURES FOR EACH COMMITTEE THAT FOLLOW THE BEST INTERNATIONAL STANDARDS. IN ADDITION, THE SECRETARIAT OVERSEES THAT IT IS CARRIED OUT IN ACCORDANCE WITH NNF'S RULES AND POLICIES, INCLUDING THE RULES OF ELIGIBILITY TO ENSURE FULL IMPARTIALITY OF COMMITTEE MEMBERS. UNDER THESE RULES OF ELIGIBILITY, INDIVIDUALS ARE INELIGIBLE TO ASSESS AN APPLICATION IF THEY HAVE A PERSONAL, PROFESSIONAL, OR FINANCIAL INTEREST IN THE OUTCOME OF AN APPLICATION: FOR EXAMPLE, IF THE MEMBER SUPERVISES THE APPLICANT; IS EMPLOYED IN THE SCIENTIFIC UNIT OF WHICH THE APPLICANT IS A LEADER; OR IF THE MEMBER HAS A FAMILY RELATIONSHIP TO THE APPLICANT. NNF SYSTEMATICALLY COLLECTS DATA ON THE ACTIVITIES, OUTPUTS AND OUTCOMES OF EACH GRANT RECIPIENT'S PROJECT THROUGH ITS TWO ONLINE DATA-COLLECTION AND REPORTING SYSTEMS RESEARCHFISH AND FOUNDGOOD. THE TWO SYSTEMS ENABLE NNF TO SYSTEMATICALLY MONITOR THE ACTIVITIES OF ITS GRANT RECIPIENTS AND THE RESULTS OF THEIR WORK. SINCE ALL GRANT RECIPIENTS REPORT ANNUALLY, NNF HAS DATA ON THE FULL POPULATION WITH NO ATTRITION. IT IS MANDATORY FOR ALL GRANT RECIPIENTS TO USE NNF'S WEB-BASED SURVEY SYSTEMS (RESEARCHFISH AND FOUNDGOOD) FOR REPORTING ON ACTIVITIES AND RESULTS RELEVANT TO THEIR GRANT. THE GRANT RECIPIENTS REPORT ANNUALLY FOR THE DURATION OF THE PROJECT AND 1-5 YEARS AFTER THE PROJECT ENDS. NNF USES DATA IN EVALUATING TYPES OF GRANTS AND FUNDING INSTRUMENTS - WHAT WORKS AND WHAT DOES NOT WORK - TO SUPPORT THE BOARD IN ITS DECISIONS ON GRANT POLICY AND GRANT-AWARDING.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: PRESIDENT AND FELLOWS OF HARVARD COLLEGE
THE UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS MEDICAL BRANCH AT GALVESTON
REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DAVIS CAMPUS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: NNF'S GRANTS ARE PARTLY AWARDED THROUGH OPEN CALLS AND PARTLY THROUGH STRATEGIC ONE-OFF INITIATIVES THAT ARE APPROVED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. NNF AWARDS GRANTS IN OPEN COMPETITION WITHIN THE SEVEN GRANT-AWARDING FOCUS STRATEGIES. ALL GRANTS AWARDED IN OPEN COMPETITION ARE PEER REVIEWED BY COMMITTEES THAT HAVE BEEN ESTABLISHED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THESE 24 COMMITTEES ARE COMPRISED OF 5 TO 12 MEMBERS WHO ARE INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED EXPERTS IN THEIR FIELD, AND WHO EVALUATE THE GRANTS BASED ON QUALITY, NOVELTY, POTENTIAL IMPACT, BUDGET, AND QUALIFICATIONS OF THE APPLICANT. FOR A LIST OF THESE COMMITTEES, SEE HTTPS://NOVONORDISKFONDEN.DK/EN/COMMITTEES. EACH COMMITTEE EVALUATES EACH APPLICATION ASSIGNED TO IT VERY CAREFULLY. FIRST, THE MEMBERS OF THE COMMITTEE EVALUATE THE APPLICATIONS INDIVIDUALLY AND GIVE THEM A SCORE. AFTER THIS, THE COMMITTEE MEETS TO DISCUSS THE HIGHEST-RANKING APPLICATIONS TO REACH CONSENSUS ON GRANTING RECOMMENDATIONS. THE EVALUATION PROCESS OF SOME CALLS MAY INCLUDE AN ADDITIONAL ELABORATED APPLICATION, A PERSONAL INTERVIEW WITH THE APPLICANT, OR THE USE OF FURTHER EXPERT PEER REVIEW. NNF POSTS ITS OPEN COMPETITION CALLS FOR APPLICATIONS HERE - HTTPS://NOVONORDISKFONDEN.DK/EN/GRANT/ - AND INTERESTED APPLICANTS APPLY ON-LINE USING NORMA, NNF'S APPLICATION AND GRANT MANAGEMENT SYSTEM. THE ENTIRE EVALUATION PROCESS IS CLOSELY MONITORED BY NNF'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS, WHICH HAS SET RULES OF PROCEDURES FOR EACH COMMITTEE THAT FOLLOW THE BEST INTERNATIONAL STANDARDS. IN ADDITION, THE SECRETARIAT OVERSEES THAT IT IS CARRIED OUT IN ACCORDANCE WITH NNF'S RULES AND POLICIES, INCLUDING THE RULES OF ELIGIBILITY TO ENSURE FULL IMPARTIALITY OF COMMITTEE MEMBERS. UNDER THESE RULES OF ELIGIBILITY, INDIVIDUALS ARE INELIGIBLE TO ASSESS AN APPLICATION IF THEY HAVE A PERSONAL, PROFESSIONAL, OR FINANCIAL INTEREST IN THE OUTCOME OF AN APPLICATION: FOR EXAMPLE, IF THE MEMBER SUPERVISES THE APPLICANT; IS EMPLOYED IN THE SCIENTIFIC UNIT OF WHICH THE APPLICANT IS A LEADER; OR IF THE MEMBER HAS A FAMILY RELATIONSHIP TO THE APPLICANT. NNF SYSTEMATICALLY COLLECTS DATA ON THE ACTIVITIES, OUTPUTS AND OUTCOMES OF EACH GRANT RECIPIENT'S PROJECT THROUGH ITS TWO ONLINE DATA-COLLECTION AND REPORTING SYSTEMS RESEARCHFISH AND FOUNDGOOD. THE TWO SYSTEMS ENABLE NNF TO SYSTEMATICALLY MONITOR THE ACTIVITIES OF ITS GRANT RECIPIENTS AND THE RESULTS OF THEIR WORK. SINCE ALL GRANT RECIPIENTS REPORT ANNUALLY, NNF HAS DATA ON THE FULL POPULATION WITH NO ATTRITION. IT IS MANDATORY FOR ALL GRANT RECIPIENTS TO USE NNF'S WEB-BASED SURVEY SYSTEMS (RESEARCHFISH AND FOUNDGOOD) FOR REPORTING ON ACTIVITIES AND RESULTS RELEVANT TO THEIR GRANT. THE GRANT RECIPIENTS REPORT ANNUALLY FOR THE DURATION OF THE PROJECT AND 1-5 YEARS AFTER THE PROJECT ENDS. NNF USES DATA IN EVALUATING TYPES OF GRANTS AND FUNDING INSTRUMENTS - WHAT WORKS AND WHAT DOES NOT WORK - TO SUPPORT THE BOARD IN ITS DECISIONS ON GRANT POLICY AND GRANT-AWARDING.

AS OF 2024, NNF'S GRANTMAKING STRATEGY HAS THREE PILLARS: HEALTH, SUSTAINABILITY, AND THE LIFE SCIENCE ECOSYSTEM. - HEALTH - THE FOUNDATION AIMS TO IMPROVE THE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT OF DISEASES THAT THREATEN GLOBAL HEALTH AS WELL AS PROVIDE MORE EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES FOR A HEALTHY LIFE AND TREATMENT. IN ADDITION TO STRENGTHENING PREVENTION AND MANAGEMENT OF CARDIOMETABOLIC DISEASES, THE FOUNDATION WILL FOCUS ON DECREASING THE BURDEN AND THREAT OF INFECTIOUS DISEASES, ON ADVANCING AND APPLYING REGENERATIVE MEDICINE AND REDUCING INEQUITY IN HEALTH. - SUSTAINABILITY - THE FOUNDATION AIMS TO ADVANCE KNOWLEDGE AND SOLUTIONS TO SUPPORT THE GREEN TRANSITION IN SOCIETY. STRATEGIC THEMES INCLUDE THE DEVELOPMENT OF SUSTAINABLE AND HIGH-YIELD AGRICULTURE, SUSTAINABLE FOOD FOR HEALTHY DIETS, SCALABLE CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION TECHNOLOGIES AS WELL AS SUPPORTING THE GREEN TRANSITION IN SOCIETY. - LIFE SCIENCE ECOSYSTEM - THE FOUNDATION'S MISSION IS TO INVEST IN SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, EDUCATION AND INNOVATION TO ENABLE A WORLD CLASS LIFE SCIENCE ECOSYSTEM. STRATEGIC THEMES INCLUDE ADVANCING FUNDAMENTAL RESEARCH, ENABLING RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURES AND TECHNOLOGIES, STRENGTHENING THE TRANSLATIONAL CAPACITY AND SOCIETAL IMPACT OF LIFE SCIENCE AND FURTHERING EDUCATION AND SCIENCE CAPITAL.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY KPMG, NNF'S OUTSIDE ACCOUNTING FIRM, AND REVIEWED BY NNF'S INTERNAL TAX TEAM. THEN, THE FORM 990 IS APPROVED BY MANAGEMENT AND FILED AS REQUIRED WITH THE IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ANY ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST SHALL BE REPORTED TO THE EMPLOYEE'S MANAGER AS WELL AS THEIR P&O PARTNER OR THE NNF COMPLIANCE OFFICER IMMEDIATELY UPON DISCOVERY. MANAGERS AND THE NNF COMPLIANCE OFFICER ARE TO ENSURE NO EMPLOYEES WITH ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST ARE INVOLVED IN ANY RELEVANT DECISION-MAKING PROCESSES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: COMPENSATION REVIEW & APPROVAL PROCESS - DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS IN ESTABLISHING THE COMPENSATION OF THE ORGANIZATION'S DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS, INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION CONSULTANTS ARE UTILIZED, COMPENSATION STUDIES ARE COMPLETED TO GATHER COMPARATIVE DATA, PERSONS WITH A CONFLICT INTEREST REGARDING THE COMPENSATION ARRANGEMENTS AT ISSUE ARE NOT INVOLVED IN THE DECISION MAKING PROCESS, AND AMOUNTS ARE REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. IN ADDITION, THERE IS CONTEMPORANEOUS DOCUMENTATION AND RECORD KEEPING FOR DELIBERATIONS AND DECISIONS REGARDING THE COMPENSATION ARRANGEMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC THROUGH THE ORGANIZATION'S WEBSITE AS WELL AS UPON REQUEST.

NOVO NORDISK FONDEN (NNF) HAS DISCLOSED THE NAMES AND COMPENSATION OF ALL OF ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, AND EMPLOYEES WITH SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE OVER ITS AFFAIRS WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 4958(F)(1)(A). IT CANNOT, HOWEVER, PUBLICLY DISCLOSE THE COMPENSATION OF CERTAIN SUBJECT MATTER EXPERTS WHO FALL WITHIN THE INSTRUCTIONS' DEFINITIONS OF 'HIGHLY COMPENSATED EMPLOYEES' BECAUSE THIS INFORMATION SHOULD NOT BE PUBLICLY DISCLOSED PURSUANT TO THE DATA PROTECTION REGULATIONS WITHIN THE EUROPEAN UNION. THESE EXPERTS DO NOT HAVE SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE OR CONTROL OVER NNF AND THEIR COMPENSATION IS REASONABLE COMPENSATION NEGOTIATED AT ARM'S LENGTH AND CAN BE PROVIDED TO THE IRS UPON REQUEST.
